title: Define density-aware adaptive SF selection rules and pseudocode
design: Density-aware SF selection: Use neighbor_count from RPL parent list + gradient cache, RSSI/SNR EMA from RfHealthMetrics, PER from tx_failures/packets_tx. Thresholds: HIGH_DENSITY=8 neighbors, LOW=3. PER_THRESHOLD=20%, SF_STEP=1. estimate_density = neighbor_count + (PER>10?2:0) + (avg_rssi < -90?1:0). Prioritize capacity (lower SF) in low density, robustness (higher SF) in high density or high PER. Integrate with adaptive_sf_from_snr fallback. Must match test vectors in ccp15.json. 3 codereview passes required per workflow rule.
notes: Spec location: expand Interference Mitigation Algorithm section in spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md. Update pseudocode, add normative MUST/SHOULD, define all helper functions with exact formulas. Update test/vectors if new vectors needed. Reference rf_health.rs:64 and lora_l2.c CCA.
close_reason: Density-aware adaptive SF rules, SNR table, normative pseudocode with vector-matching logic, load_factor override, and embedded notes added as §2a.7.2 in spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md (lines ~293-360). 6 codereview passes completed (3 initial P2 findings addressed in final edit, final 3 passes P4/opinion only with convergence). Matches parent epic requirements and ccp_load_balancing.json vectors. Follow-up impl updates in Rust/Zephyr/Python filed via earlier reviews.
